discoveries.summary: Content sync page mentions 'sufficient permissions to synchronize forms' but does not document the Synchronize ACL permission requirement for page synchronization (Read + Synchronize on the page AND all ancestor pages)
discoveries.evidence: code-analysis permissionSummary.synchronize shows: 'READ + SYNCHRONIZE on the page AND all ancestor pages up the tree'. content-sync.md line 66 only mentions 'if you have sufficient permissions to synchronize forms' without specifics.
discoveries.suggestedAction: Add a brief permission note to the Content sync 'Pages' section referencing the Synchronize page permission, or cross-reference to Page permission management.

discoveries.summary: UI page permission checks documentation (developer customization page) describes the application-level permission framework but does not reference or explain the page-level ACL permission layer or how it interacts with the [PageCommand] attribute
discoveries.evidence: doc-index shows ui-page-permission-checks.md with headings about application permissions, workspace permissions, but no section on page ACL permissions. AREA-004 code analysis shows the two-layer model where PageInvokerBase checks [PageCommand] permission first, then page-specific code checks ACLs.
discoveries.suggestedAction: Consider adding a note or cross-reference to the UI page permission checks page explaining that website channel pages have an additional ACL-based permission layer beyond application permissions.

discoveries.summary: Headless content page has Publish/Unpublish/Delete sections with no permission mentions — same gap pattern as website page operations, but headless items use workspace permissions (not ACL page permissions)
discoveries.evidence: doc-index shows headless-content.md with headings: 'Publish headless items', 'Unpublish headless items', 'Delete headless items'. Code analysis focused on web page ACL permissions (AREA-001) which don't apply to headless items. Headless items use workspace-scoped application permissions instead.
discoveries.suggestedAction: Out of scope for DOC-3167 (which focuses on page permissions/ACLs). Consider a separate task to add permission cross-references to headless content operations.

discoveries.summary: Content items (Content Hub) page has Publish/Create/Delete/Synchronize sections with no permission mentions — reusable content items use workspace permissions, not page ACLs
discoveries.evidence: doc-index shows content-items.md with headings including 'Publish content items', 'Create content items', 'Delete content items', 'Synchronize content items'. These operations use workspace-scoped application permissions (not page ACLs).
discoveries.suggestedAction: Out of scope for DOC-3167. Consider a separate task to add permission cross-references to Content Hub operations.

discoveries.summary: Clone page operation (Create pages → Clone a page) mentions that 'page permissions are not cloned' but does not mention what permissions are needed TO clone — code analysis shows Read on source + Read + Create on target parent
discoveries.evidence: create-pages.md line 31 mentions 'page permissions are not cloned' but the Clone procedure steps have no permission prerequisite. permissionSummary.clonePage: 'READ on the source page AND READ + CREATE on the target parent page'.
discoveries.suggestedAction: Add a brief permission note to the Clone section, or ensure the new 'Page permissions for creating pages' section on Page permission management mentions cloning as a related operation.
